title: Add Git Subtree Support to GMD
instructions: Add git subtree management capabilities to gmd-commit, allowing users to manage subtrees alongside submodules. Subtrees are alternative to submodules where external repos are merged into subdirectories with full history.

1784084875.817157.instruction: Create gmd/gui/__init__.py and gmd/gui/main.py with Tkinter application class. Set up main window with horizontal layout, title, minimum size, and basic frame structure (left panel for source, right panel for destination, bottom panel for controls).

log.text: Build started

Created gmd/gui/__init__.py with package exports and gmd/gui/main.py with GMDGuiApp class. Main window has horizontal layout with title, minimum size 1200x700, PanedWindow for resizable left/right panels, menu bar stubs, status bar with progress bar, and placeholder methods for all functionality.

1784084898.4503944.instruction: Create menu bar with File menu (Open Source, Open Destination, Exit), Commands menu (Scan, Compare, Preview, Sync, Backup, Diff), and Help menu (Documentation, About). Connect menu items to stub methods.

log.text: Menu bar already complete from Task 1. Verified File menu has Open Source, Open Destination, Exit; Commands menu has Scan, Compare, Preview, Sync, Diff, Backup; Help menu has Documentation, About. All items connected to methods.

1784084901.602261.instruction: Create two Treeview panels (left for source, right for destination) displaying directory structure with columns for filename, size, modified date. Add scrollbars, enable horizontal layout with PanedWindow. Implement directory loading and display.

log.text: Created two Treeview panels (left_tree and right_tree) with columns for Name, Size, Modified, Status. Added vertical and horizontal scrollbars for both panels. Treeviews are placed inside PanedWindow for resizable horizontal layout. Implemented _load_directory_to_tree method to populate trees, _populate_from_scan for scan-based population, _populate_simple for basic directory listing. Both _open_source and _open_dest now call _load_directory_to_tree to display directory contents. Scan directories loads data into trees with file count, size, and modification time.

1784084904.3799584.instruction: Add right-click (Button-3) context menu to both Treeview panels. Include "Open in File Browser" command that launches system file manager at selected path. Add platform-specific implementations for Windows, macOS, Linux.

log.text: Added right-click context menu to both Treeview panels with _setup_context_menu method. Context menu includes "Open in File Browser" and "Refresh" commands. _open_in_file_browser builds full path from selected tree item. _launch_file_manager has platform-specific implementations: Windows (os.startfile), macOS (open command), Linux (xdg-open, nautilus, dolphin, thunar, pcmanfm with fallback). Also added _refresh_tree method to reload directory contents.

1784084907.153305.instruction: Implement automatic scan when directories are selected. Integrate DirectoryScanner and FileComparator from gmd.core. Show scan progress in status bar. Display file comparison results with color coding (green for same, yellow for different, red for missing).

log.text: Implemented automatic scan when directories are selected via _auto_scan() calling _scan_directories(). Added color tags configuration (same=green, different=yellow, missing=red, extra=blue) with _configure_tree_tags(). Scan shows progress with indeterminate progress bar. _compare_directories() integrates FileComparator with auto-compare after scan. _apply_comparison_colors() and _colorize_tree() apply color coding to tree items based on comparison results (MISSING=red, UPDATE=yellow, EXISTS=green, EXTRA=blue). Status bar shows scan and comparison progress with file counts.

1784084909.6607797.instruction: Create bottom panel with action buttons: Preview, Sync, Diff, Backup, Cancel. Add exclude patterns entry field with label. Include direction selector (Source→Dest, Dest→Source, Bidirectional). Connect buttons to core functionality.

log.text: Created complete bottom control panel with _create_control_panel method. Includes: direction selector with 3 radio buttons (Source→Destination, Destination→Source, Bidirectional), excludes entry field with label and hint text, action buttons (Preview, Synchronize, Show Diff, Backup, Cancel) with proper spacing and separator. All buttons connected to functionality: Preview shows changes in popup window, Synchronize performs merge with confirmation dialog, Show Diff displays file differences, Backup creates destination backup, Cancel stops operation. Fixed code duplication issue by rewriting complete file.

1784084912.3406227.instruction: Add progress bar widget to bottom of window. Create status bar showing current operation, file counts (scanned, different, same). Update progress during scan, compare, and sync operations. Show elapsed time.

log.text: Enhanced status bar with elapsed time tracking. Added _start_timer(), _update_timer(), _stop_timer() methods using time.time() and tkinter after(). Status bar now shows: status message, file counts (Files, Same, Diff, Missing), elapsed time, and progress bar. Timer starts/stops during scan, compare, and sync operations. File counts update via _update_counts() method after comparison.

1784084918.4583485.instruction: Find and integrate icons for file types (folder, file, modified, missing, same). Use tkinter.PhotoImage or Pillow for icon support. Add icons to Treeview items. Style buttons with icons if available. Set application icon.

1784084922.1473644.instruction: Create gmd-gui entry point script. Update setup.py with gui dependencies and entry point. Update gmd/gui/__init__.py exports. Test integration with existing gmd.core modules. Create documentation for GUI usage.

1784260209.2207522.instruction: Create a `Subtree` dataclass in gmd/cli/commit.py (similar to Submodule) with name, path, url, and prefix attributes. Add a method `detect_subtrees()` to GitSubmoduleManager class that detects subtrees by parsing `git log` output or checking `.git/config` for subtree merge commits. Subtrees are identified by commits with "git-subtree-dir:" in the message or by checking git config for subtree entries.

log.text: Build started

Created Subtree dataclass with name, path, url, and prefix attributes. Added detect_subtrees() method to GitSubmoduleManager that searches git log for subtree merge commits (git-subtree-dir: and git-subtree-url:), parses commit messages to extract subtree information, and returns a list of unique Subtree objects. Fixed file corruption by rewriting complete commit.py file.

1784260211.6381989.instruction: Add a `get_subtree_status()` method to check if a subtree directory has uncommitted changes. Similar to `get_submodule_status()`, it should run `git status --porcelain` in the subtree path and return (status, details) tuple with "clean", "modified", or "error" status.

log.text: Added get_subtree_status() method to GitSubmoduleManager class. The method runs git status --porcelain on the subtree's prefix path (relative to main repo) and returns (status, details) tuple. Status values are "clean", "modified", or "error". Details include counts of modified/added/deleted files. Uses the subtree's prefix attribute to check status in the main repository context.

1784260217.3345308.instruction: Add methods to GitSubmoduleManager for subtree operations: `subtree_pull()` to fetch and merge updates from remote, and `subtree_push()` to push local changes back to remote. These should use `git subtree pull` and `git subtree push` commands with proper prefix and remote URL handling.

log.text: Added subtree_pull() and subtree_push() methods to GitSubmoduleManager class. subtree_pull() uses 'git subtree pull' with --squash option and prefix/remote URL to fetch and merge updates. subtree_push() uses 'git subtree push' with prefix/remote URL to push local changes back to remote. Both methods return (success, message) tuples and support dry_run mode. Methods handle missing URL/prefix validation and include proper error handling with timeouts.

1784260220.1887429.instruction: Update the gmd-commit CLI in commit.py to add a `--subtrees` flag that enables subtree mode. Also add `--operation` choices to include "pull" and "push" for subtree-specific operations. Update the main() function to handle subtree operations alongside existing submodule operations.

log.text: Updated gmd-commit CLI with --subtrees flag to enable subtree mode. Added "pull" operation choice alongside existing operations. Updated main() function to detect mode (submodule vs subtree) based on --subtrees flag. Added process_subtree() method to handle subtree-specific operations (status, pull, push). Updated docstring to mention subtrees. Modified processing logic to use appropriate method based on mode.

Task 13 completed: Added --subtrees flag to CLI, added "pull" and "push" operations for subtree-specific commands, updated main() function to handle both submodule and subtree modes with appropriate processing logic. The CLI now supports: gmd-commit --subtrees --operation status/pull/push.

Task 13 verified complete. The CLI now includes: --subtrees flag to enable subtree mode, "pull" and "push" operations added to click.Choice, main() function properly handles both submodule and subtree modes with process_subtree() method for subtree operations. CLI options at lines 300-315 show all required flags and operations.

1784260224.014986.instruction: Add a SubtreeConfig class to gmd/config/schema.py with settings like auto_pull, squash, and message_prefix. Update GMDConfig to include a subtree field with default SubtreeConfig. This allows users to configure subtree behavior in config files.

log.text: Task 14 verified complete. Added SubtreeConfig class with settings: auto_pull (bool, default False), squash (bool, default True), message_prefix (str, default "[subtree] "), default_branch (str, default "main"). Added field_validator to ensure message_prefix ends with space. Updated GMDConfig to include subtree field with default SubtreeConfig factory. Configuration schema now supports subtree behavior customization in JSON/YAML config files.

1784260226.8305998.instruction: Add a new CLI option `--detect-subtrees` that automatically finds all subtrees in the repository. Create a helper function that runs `git log --all --oneline --grep="git-subtree-dir:"` to find subtree merge commits and extracts directory paths and remote URLs.

log.text: Task 15 verified complete. Added --detect-subtrees CLI option as a click flag. The detect_subtrees() method in GitSubmoduleManager runs git log --all --oneline --grep="git-subtree-dir:" to find subtree merge commits, then parses each commit with git show to extract git-subtree-dir and git-subtree-url values. Results are displayed with formatter.info() showing name, prefix, and URL for each detected subtree before exiting.

1784260229.4195373.instruction: Update the OutputFormatter classes in gmd/output/ to handle subtree-specific output. Add methods like `subtree_status()` and `subtree_operation()` similar to existing submodule methods. Ensure all formatters (color, plain, json) support subtree display.

log.text: Task 16 completed. Updated all output formatters with subtree-specific methods: console.py - subtree_status() and subtree_operation() with tree emoji (🌲) to distinguish from submodules; plain.py - subtree_status() and subtree_operation() with [SUBTREE:] prefix; json_fmt.py - subtree_status() and subtree_operation() with item_type field set to "subtree"; formatter.py - dispatcher methods for all formatters. All formatters now support subtree display alongside existing submodule methods.

1784260231.9962807.instruction: Create example configuration files in gmd/config/ showing subtree settings: commit.subtree.example.json and commit.subtree.example.yaml. Include settings for auto_pull, squash merges, and message_prefix to demonstrate subtree configuration options.

log.text: Created example configuration files for subtree support: commit.subtree.example.json with JSON format showing all subtree settings (auto_pull, squash, message_prefix, default_branch); commit.subtree.example.yaml with YAML format including comments explaining each option. Both files demonstrate subtree configuration alongside existing commit and output settings.

1784260235.5008194.instruction: Update README.md to document subtree support alongside submodules. Add a new section explaining the difference between submodules and subtrees, with examples of subtree commands. Also update CHANGELOG.md to document the new subtree feature.

log.text: Task 18 completed. Updated README.md with: subtree support section explaining difference between submodules and subtrees with comparison table; subtree command examples (detect, status, pull, push, dry-run); updated gmd-commit features list to include subtree support; updated CLI reference table with --subtrees and --detect-subtrees options. Updated CHANGELOG.md with: new subtree support section under [Unreleased] documenting all subtree features; updated Changed section to mention README and CLI updates.

1784260237.8536434.instruction: Create integration tests in tests/ for subtree functionality. Test subtree detection, pull, and push operations. Mock git commands to avoid requiring actual git repositories in tests. Verify that subtree operations are properly integrated with the existing commit workflow.

log.text: Task 19 completed. Created comprehensive integration tests in tests/test_subtree.py with 6 test classes: TestSubtreeDetection (empty, single, multiple, no URL), TestSubtreeStatus (clean, modified, error), TestSubtreeOperations (pull success, dry-run, missing URL, push success/failure), TestSubtreeProcessing (status, pull, push operations), TestSubtreeConfig (defaults, validation), TestSubtreeIntegration (mode detection, method coexistence). All tests use mocked subprocess.run to avoid requiring actual git repositories. Tests verify subtree operations integrate properly with existing commit workflow without interfering with submodule functionality.

Task 19 verified complete. Integration tests file tests/test_subtree.py is properly structured with all required test coverage: subtree detection (empty, single, multiple, no URL scenarios), subtree status checking (clean, modified, error states), subtree operations (pull success/failure, dry-run, missing URL, push success/failure), subtree processing workflow (status, pull, push operations), subtree configuration validation, and integration tests verifying subtree mode doesn't interfere with submodule functionality. All tests use unittest.mock.patch to mock subprocess.run calls, avoiding need for actual git repositories.
